pi_items.source: setup_task.sh confirms path creation and export_result.sh checks this path
setup_task.sh confirms directory creation at this path
setup_task.sh line 37 and Docker Hub official nginx repository confirm this tag exists at https://hub.docker.com/_/nginx/
setup_task.sh line 43 installs curl into webserver as unauthorized drift; nginx:1.24-alpine is Alpine-based minimal image without curl by default per https://www.shellhacks.com/alpine-install-curl/
setup_task.sh line 55 and Docker Hub official python repository confirm this tag exists at https://hub.docker.com/_/python
setup_task.sh line 60 installs debugpy via pip; python:3.11-slim is Debian bookworm-slim based and does not include debugpy by default per https://pypi.org/project/debugpy/
setup_task.sh line 71 and Docker Hub alpine repository confirm this tag exists at https://hub.docker.com/_/alpine (Alpine 3.19 released December 2023)
verifier.py line 119 retrieves pass_threshold from metadata and verifier uses >= comparison; scoring is out of 100 points total (25 report + 15 configs + 15 originals + 45 clean)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses three official Docker images: nginx:1.24-alpine (Alpine-based minimal image without curl by default), python:3.11-slim (Debian bookworm-slim without debugpy by default), and alpine:3.19 (minimal Alpine Linux from December 2023 release). The task verifies agent performance with a scoring rubric out of 100 points (report existence and content: 25 points, config extraction: 15 points, stopping original containers: 15 points, deploying clean replacement containers: 45 points) with a pass threshold of 60 points. Agents must identify unauthorized drift (curl in webserver, debugpy in appserver) and legitimate configuration changes, extract preserved configs to /home/ga/projects/container-configs/, document findings in /home/ga/Desktop/drift_report.txt, and redeploy containers from clean base images.
